_meta.purpose: Regression fixture for the v0.7.0 polish-quality investigation + v0.7.4 punctuation-preservation fix + v0.7.5 voice-edit mode.
_meta.context: These are the failure modes that pushed home-stt back from Qwen2.5-1.5B to Qwen3-4B-Instruct-2507 as the default polish model. Each case captures one class of error that smaller / less-instruction-following models produced, with assertion rules the *correct* (current) polish output must satisfy. v0.7.4 added punctuation_preservation cases after live log evidence showed the asymmetric prompt (補標點 but no 禁刪標點) let the model strip 。between sentences. v0.7.5 added voice_edit cases (mode='edit') for the ⌥+E feature — asserts language-adaptive output (preserve selection language unless instruction overrides) + technical term preservation under instruction-driven rewrites.
_meta.source: README.md §'v0.7.x 效能與品質投資紀錄' + commits 676553a, 3d7ae7d, 23c8d07. Punctuation cases from %TEMP%/stt-daemon.log analysis on 2026-05-24.

schema.category: one of: english_keyword | identifier_fidelity | semantic_fidelity | subject_preservation | overformalization | no_english_translation | filler_removal | repetition_fix | punctuation_preservation | voice_edit_language | voice_edit_technical
schema.input: polish mode: raw ASR output (post-OpenCC TW, pre-polish)
schema.selection: edit mode: the text the user had selected when triggering voice-edit
schema.instruction: edit mode: the spoken instruction transcribed by ASR (post-OpenCC TW)
schema.must_contain: substrings the polished/edited output MUST contain (English keywords, identifiers, etc.)
schema.must_not_contain: substrings that MUST NOT appear (translations, wrong subjects, fact reversals, etc.)
schema.max_edit_ratio: optional (polish-friendly): ceiling on Levenshtein distance / input length. 0.3 = output should differ by ≤30% chars from input. Catches over-rewrites.
schema.max_length_ratio: optional (edit-friendly): ceiling on len(output) / len(selection). Used by shorten cases (0.6 = output ≤ 60% of input length).

_meta.running: pytest tests/test_polish_bench.py — requires actual polish model loaded (~8 GB VRAM on Win, ~4 GB RSS on Mac). Skip-by-default in CI.

cases.input: 幫我 review 這個 commit 然後 push 到 main
cases.note: Qwen2.5-1.5B silently swapped commit→push (wrong verb production bug). Qwen3-4B preserves all English tech verbs.

cases.input: 呃這個 Python function 的 async 部分需要改
cases.note: English tech keywords must not be translated, even though OpenCC has 函式 in its TW vocab dict — polish should respect verbatim English.

cases.input: 把 _USE_TORCH_COMPILE 設成 True 試試
cases.note: Qwen2.5-0.5B dropped the underscore prefix. Identifiers are opaque tokens — must be preserved byte-for-byte. must_not_contain uses LEADING SPACE before USE_ to detect underscore stripping specifically: input has ' _USE_...' (space-underscore-U), so a model that preserves '_' produces no ' U' substring (it's ' _U'); a model that strips '_' produces ' U' which matches. v0.7.4 fixture fix: prior 'USE_TORCH_COMPILE 設成' substring was a superstring of '_USE_TORCH_COMPILE 設成' and false-positived even when '_' was preserved.

cases.input: 改一下 build_polisher 跟 build_backend_with_fallback 這兩個
cases.note: snake_case identifiers must survive polish unchanged.

cases.input: INT4 量化在這台機器上反而更慢
cases.note: Qwen2.5-1.5B flipped 更慢→更快 (semantic reversal hallucination). Polish must never invert the speaker's claim.

cases.input: polish 從 4.26 秒降到 1.9 秒
cases.note: Numerical facts must survive — silent number mutation is fatal for technical dictation.

cases.input: 嗯幫我改一下這段程式碼
cases.note: Qwen2.5-0.5B swapped 幫我→幫你 (subject swap). Polish must preserve pronoun roles.

cases.input: 你那邊的 daemon 跟我這邊的版本應該一樣
cases.note: Both 你 and 我 must survive — small models sometimes collapse them.

cases.input: 我想要解決這個 latency 問題
cases.note: Qwen2.5-1.5B over-formalised 想要解決→目的是為了解決. Polish should preserve conversational tone.

cases.input: 這個應該很容易實作
cases.note: Qwen2.5-1.5B swapped 實作→實現 (mainland register). TW vocab should be preserved.

cases.input: 找不到 prebuilt wheel 只好自己 compile
cases.note: Qwen2.5-1.5B translated prebuilt wheel→預建的輪子 (literal noun translation). English jargon must stay English.

cases.input: PyTorch 跟 transformers 都要先裝好
cases.note: Library / brand names must survive verbatim.

cases.input: 呃我覺得這個 design 還可以
cases.note: Core polish duty: remove 呃/嗯/那個/就是/然後 fillers.

cases.input: 然後就是那個 commit 之後直接 push
cases.note: Multi-filler removal — 然後 + 就是 + 那個 stacked, all must go.

cases.input: 我我我覺得這樣比較好
cases.note: Immediate-repetition fix — 我我我→我.

cases.input: 這個這個那個 function 寫得不太對
cases.note: Repeated demonstratives + filler before noun, all must collapse.

cases.input: 呃所以我剛剛在想說那個 polish 的 prompt 是不是太長了然後 latency 就會上升
cases.note: Realistic mid-length dictation — preserve technical content, remove the dictation-ish filler pattern.

cases.input: 好
cases.note: v0.7.2 O7: short responses like 「好」 must now reach polish (they were silent-rejected by MIN_AUDIO_SEC=0.3 before). Already-clean input should pass through near-untouched.

cases.input: 我剛剛在測試這個工具的過程中，發現了一個小問題。我發現中文的輸出都沒有標點符號。
cases.note: v0.7.4 trigger case (verbatim from %TEMP%/stt-daemon.log 2026-05-24). The pre-v0.7.4 prompt's asymmetric rule (補必要標點 but no 禁刪標點) let Qwen3-4B drop the inter-sentence「。」and merge two sentences with a space. Both periods must survive — they delimit speaker intent.

cases.input: 目前我輸出的文字都是透過這個工具來輸出。你可以發現前面的句子是沒有標點符號，後面又突然出現標點符號，請幫我分析一下。
cases.note: Longer multi-sentence case (pre-v0.7.4 polish stripped ALL period+comma on this input). Worst affected pattern in the live log; if this passes the prompt fix is working.

cases.input: 讓我們先完成。
cases.note: Even single-sentence inputs lost their trailing「。」pre-v0.7.4 (log: 「讓我們先完成。」→「讓我們先完成」). Period must survive on shortest realistic case.

cases.input: 你可以透過 GH CLI 來確認 pipeline 的執行狀況嗎？
cases.note: ？ was already mostly preserved pre-v0.7.4 (no log regression seen), but added as defensive regression guard now that punctuation is in the bench's scope.

cases.input: 是我需要有講話有停頓，然後才會有標點符號，還是其他原因？
cases.note: Multiple ，inside one sentence + ？at end. The 然後 filler may go (allowed by prompt) but commas surrounding the kept content + the final ？ must survive. Regression guard for the prompt change not over-suppressing legitimate filler removal.

cases.selection: 我覺得這個 design 還可以
cases.note: v0.7.5 trigger: Chinese selection + Chinese instruction → output must stay Chinese (must_not_contain English phrases that'd appear if model defaulted to English). Originally also asserted 'design' English-term preservation, but observed Qwen3-4B legitimately translates design→設計 under formal-rewrite context (which is consistent with what a human editor would do for '更專業'). Tightening identifier preservation is covered by edit_technical_preserve (which asserts _USE_TORCH_COMPILE survives — a stricter case).

cases.selection: 我們需要修一下這個 bug
cases.note: Chinese selection + English instruction = explicit override → output English. Tests the 'instruction explicitly says so' branch of EDIT_PROMPT's language rule. 'bug' was already English in the selection so it must remain English in output.

English selection + Chinese instruction = explicit override → output Chinese. Reverse direction of edit_translate_to_en. 'bug' may or may not be translated (it's loanword in TW Chinese), so we don't assert on it — we assert on the structural English words being gone.

Substantive rewrite expected — 'formality change' is exactly what voice-edit is for. '喂' is informal, MUST be removed. No max_edit_ratio (rewrite IS expected). No length constraint because the formal version could be either shorter or longer.

cases.selection: 這個東西其實非常非常的複雜然後我覺得我們應該要花更多時間去研究
cases.note: Length-bound assertion: output ≤ 60% of input length. Tests the 'shorten' instruction class — common voice-edit use case. The repeated '非常非常' is the obvious shortening target; must be collapsed.

Identifier + boolean must survive any instruction-driven rewrite. _USE_TORCH_COMPILE is the same identifier-fidelity case as id_underscore_prefix but tested under edit-mode rewrite (which is more aggressive than polish minimum-edit). EDIT_PROMPT's '保留原本的英文技術詞彙與識別字' rule guards this.
